reason: The Artin-Wedderburn theorem is invoked by name as the sole justification for the decomposition, but it is not declared as an external dependency in the proof framework. For traceability and rigor, this well-known theorem should be registered as an external reference (e.g., with a standard citation such as Lam, 'A First Course in Noncommutative Rings', Theorem 3.5, or Kadison-Ringrose Vol. I, Theorem 6.6.5 for the C*-algebraic version). Similarly, the implicit use of the Gelfand-Mazur theorem (that C is the only finite-dimensional complex division algebra, needed to reduce division rings to C in the Artin-Wedderburn decomposition) should be noted.
